title: Sr. AI Engineer, Labs
company_focus: Automating home design and construction with AI
description:
About Us

Higharc is a VC-backed startup that is changing how new
homes are designed and built. Join a founding team who’ve
shipped products for Autodesk, Electronic Arts, Nike, and
Apple. We have raised a total of $83M with support from top-
notch venture capital firms and more than 18 strategic
investors—industry leaders in construction, building
products manufacturing, and distribution.

We are hiring a Senior AI Engineer as a foundational member
of Higharc Labs. This is a role for an entrepreneurial AI
engineer who can move from problem definition to shipped
software quickly, building agent workflows that are useful,
reliable, and safe in production.

What You'll Do

You will work across product, design, ML, and engineering to
identify the right bets, build production-safe prototypes,
validate with users, and either hand off successful,
product-ready betas or sunset with shared learnings. Expect
to:

- Own AI experiments end-to-end: shape hypotheses, define
  success criteria, build, ship, measure, and iterate
- Partner with ML Engineering to productize ML prototypes and
  harden them for production, while leading graduation
  criteria across technical readiness and product readiness:
  contracts, failure modes, eval harnesses, user validation,
  reliability, controlled model updates, and cost controls
- Design agent action schemas and tool contracts that make
  model behavior predictable under real-world variance
- Build retrieval systems (RAG) for agent context:
  chunking/indexing strategies, vector search quality, and
  grounding behavior
- Build orchestration systems for agent workflows: stateful
  sessions, streaming, async job coordination, and failure
  recovery
- Build evaluation loops that combine deterministic
  validation, domain heuristics, and model-based review
- Design human-in-the-loop flows for approvals, intervention,
  and safe execution of high-impact actions
- Talk directly with users and internal stakeholders to refine
  problem framing and validate assumptions early

About You

You are excited to have unusual autonomy and greenfield
scope in a company with real revenue and customers. You
thrive in ambiguity, don't mind that some experiments get
killed, and are energized by the challenge of building
something new inside an established organization. If that
sounds like a feature and not a bug, let's go!

You have:

- 7+ years experience shipping production software, including
  customer-facing AI/LLM features
- Ownership of ambiguous, high-impact problems from concept
  through deployment and iteration
- Experience building AI agent systems: tool use, context
  management, and orchestration loops
- Experience designing and operating RAG systems, including
  vector DB-backed retrieval pipelines
- Experience defining or operating evaluation systems for non-
  deterministic AI outputs
- Strong backend systems skills, including stateful services
  and real-time/streaming workflows
- Experience managing AI costs in production (model selection,
  context budgeting, caching, and usage controls)
- Experience collaborating across research, ML platform, and
  product teams to move models into production
- Product engineering instincts: you can simplify scope and
  prioritize what creates measurable value
- Clear written and verbal communication in a remote, async
  environment

A major plus if you also bring:

- Experience as a technical founder or early engineer at a
  high-growth SaaS company
- ML depth: fine-tuning, rigorous model evaluation, and the
  ability to apply current ML/LLM research in product
  systems

While we've identified the core experience and skills
required above, please still apply if you have more or
different experience than this! We will use your previous
experience and performance across the series of interviews
to establish appropriate level within our organization in a
fair and equitable way.

Working at Higharc

Higharc has been remote first since our founding in 2018. We
offer flexible hours so you can do your best work without
missing out on life. Higharc offers competitive salaries
with significant equity, in a fast-growing, well-funded
company.  Personal healthiness is an important value for us-
we provide comprehensive medical, dental, and vision
coverage, with unlimited PTO, and meaningful
maternity/paternity leave to all U.S based employees that
are full-time. You'll also have access to other big-company
benefits such like short and long-term disability plans and
a 401K. Haven't worked remotely before? We provide a stipend
to create the ideal home office.
responsibilities: Own AI experiments end-to-end: shape hypotheses, define success criteria, build, ship, measure, and iterate
Partner with ML Engineering to productize ML prototypes and harden them for production with graduation criteria
Design agent action schemas and tool contracts for predictable model behavior under real-world variance
Build retrieval systems (RAG) for agent context: chunking/indexing strategies, vector search quality, and grounding
Build orchestration systems for agent workflows: stateful sessions, streaming, async job coordination, and failure recovery
Build evaluation loops combining deterministic validation, domain heuristics, and model-based review
Design human-in-the-loop flows for approvals, intervention, and safe execution of high-impact actions
Engage directly with users and internal stakeholders to refine problem framing and validate assumptions
use_cases: AI agent workflows for home design and construction automation
RAG-based retrieval systems for construction and building product context
Orchestration of multi-step agent actions in the homebuilding domain
Evaluation and safety assurance of non-deterministic AI outputs in production
Human-in-the-loop approval flows for high-impact construction design actions
ai_classification_reasoning:
This role is squarely ai-first: the engineer will build
agent workflows, design agent action schemas, build RAG
retrieval systems, construct orchestration systems for agent
workflows, and build evaluation loops for AI outputs. These
are all hands-on activities directly building and deploying
AI/LLM systems in production.
